vocab.example: I prefer being the driver because I like to call the shots, especially when it comes to choosing the music and the air conditioning temperature.

vocab.meaning: người ngồi sau hay chỉ đạo (thường gây khó chịu)
vocab.example: I’d much rather be at the wheel because I have a tendency to be a bit of a back-seat driver when others are navigating.
Whenever we go on long road trips, I’m always the first one to volunteer to take the wheel because I find driving quite exhilarating.
If I've had a taxing day at the office, I'd definitely prefer to be a passenger so I can just sit back and relax while someone else handles the traffic.

vocab.word: keep one's eyes on the road
vocab.example: Being the driver is quite a responsibility as you have to keep your eyes on the road at all times, which can be exhausting during rush hour.

vocab.meaning: ở vị trí kiểm soát tình hình
vocab.example: I feel much more secure when I'm in the driving seat because I trust my own instincts and reflexes more than anyone else's.

vocab.meaning: đi từ nơi này đến nơi khác
vocab.example: To be honest, I don't really care about the experience of driving; I just want to get from A to B as comfortably as possible as a passenger.

vocab.meaning: ngắm nhìn thế giới trôi qua (một cách thư thái)
vocab.example: I prefer the passenger side because it gives me a chance to watch the world go by and appreciate the scenery instead of staring at the bumper in front of me.
Being a passenger allows me to recharge my batteries by taking a short nap or listening to a podcast during the commute.

vocab.meaning: ngủ quên khi đang làm việc (nghĩa bóng) hoặc ngủ gật khi lái xe (nghĩa đen)
vocab.example: I'm terrified of falling asleep at the wheel during late-night drives, so I usually prefer to let a friend drive while I stay awake to keep them company.
